config_data._comment_2: Canonical shape for the p03-jwks-rs256-basic scenario. The axis measured is:
config_data._comment_3: (1) Tyk enforces Authorization: Bearer <jwt> on every /anything request (no Authorization -> 4xx: Tyk returns 400 'Authorization field missing', not 401 — hard-coded in mw_jwt.go and not overridable in Classic OSS)
config_data._comment_4: (2) Tyk fetches the JWKS from `jwt_source` on the private bench-net and verifies RS256 signature using the JWK whose `kid` matches the incoming token header (valid kid -> 200)
config_data._comment_5: (3) Tyk rejects a token whose `kid` header is not present in the JWKS (unknown kid -> 4xx: Tyk returns 403 'Key not authorized', again hard-coded)
config_data._comment_6: `jwt_default_policies` binds each decoded token to the permissive bench-default-policy in _policies/policies.json — without a default policy Tyk would 4xx every token with 'no session found for token user identity', even for correctly signed ones.
config_data._comment_7: `jwt_source` is base64-encoded (aHR0c...) per Tyk 5.11.1 convention: mw_jwt.go's getSecretFromURL caches the source as base64 and decodes it on every subsequent request — plain-text URLs work for the first hit then blow up with 'illegal base64 data at input byte 4' on the second. The base64-encoded URL path IS the canonical Tyk shape (see https://tyk.io/docs/basic-config-and-security/security/authentication-authorization/jwt/).
